title: feat: route flux to self-hosted Vast.ai 5090 pool with Fireworks fallback
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- Adds `callFluxWithFallback`: flux tries the self-hosted GPU pool first, any pool error (incl. empty pool) falls back to Fireworks unchanged
- `callSelfHostedServer` gains a `poolType` param (zimage default unchanged)
- `nunchaku/server.py`: env-parametrized `QUANT_MODEL_PATH` / `MAX_PIXELS`; `QUEUE_LIMIT` (default 10) sheds load with 503 → gateway falls back to Fireworks
- `setup-vast.sh`: one-command Vast provisioning incl. Cloudflare tunnel (`TUNNEL_NAME=...`); workers MUST be tunneled — Workers `fetch()` can't reach raw-IP:port origins
- `GPU_INSTANCES.md`: live Vast fleet, provision one-liner, gotchas
- Tests: pool-hit / pool-empty / pool-error fallback (3 passing)

**Already deployed to prod** (2026-07-02 11:14 UTC) — serving from instance 43575766; queue-cap verified shedding at US peak.

Incident note: a polly-suggested `AbortSignal.timeout` on the pool fetch passed vitest but broke every pool fetch in deployed workerd (silent 100% Fireworks fallback for ~1.5h). Reverted with warning comment — do not re-add; local workers-pool ≠ prod workerd.

Pilot benchmark (1×5090 FP4, $0.43/hr): 1,893 img/hr sustained, p50 2.25s @ full 1024², baseline JPEG (fixes #11582), FP4/FP8 same-seed A/B at parity.

Context: Fireworks flux spend $2,149/mo (June); 2×5090 target ≈ $620/mo + failover. Addresses #12136 fallout.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

gist.headline: The hive gets 5090s
gist.blurb: We've started routing Flux image generation to our own self-hosted RTX 5090 pool. If the queue gets too full, it seamlessly falls back to our cloud providers, keeping your generations lightning fast.
gist.summary: Flux image generation now routes to a self-hosted Vast.ai RTX 5090 pool first, with an automatic fallback to Fireworks if the queue exceeds 10 requests.
gist.impact: Users get lightning-fast 2.25s median generation times for 1024x1024 images, backed by a resilient dual-provider setup.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee and the Monitor Robot setting up a massive, glowing new GPU (RTX 5090) in a cozy server shed. The robot is plugging it in, and Polly is directing traffic (routing).
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot and a chunky monitor-head robot installing a massive, glowing retro GPU in a rustic garden server shed. The robot holds a thick cable while the bee directs traffic with a tiny glowing wand. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
